name: World War II
description: The battles, leaders, and events of the Second World War

questions.q: In which year did World War II begin in Europe?

source.notes: Germany invaded Poland on September 1, 1939. Britain and France declared war two days later.

questions.q: Which German action prompted Britain and France to declare war in September 1939?

source.notes: Germany invaded from the west on Sept 1, 1939; USSR from the east on Sept 17.

questions.q: In what month and year did Japan attack Pearl Harbor?

source.notes: December 7, 1941. FDR called it 'a date which will live in infamy.'

questions.q: Who became UK Prime Minister in May 1940 after Neville Chamberlain's resignation?

source.notes: Served as PM May 1940 - July 1945.

questions.q: In Churchill's speeches, what did his famous 'V' sign symbolize?

source.notes: Became a morale-boosting gesture across Allied nations.

questions.q: Which country suffered the most civilian deaths during World War II?

source.notes: Estimates range 14-20 million civilian deaths. Total Soviet losses over 27 million.

questions.q: What was Hitler's plan to invade Great Britain called?

source.notes: Never carried out. Abandoned after failure to gain air superiority in the Battle of Britain.

questions.q: Operation Overlord was the code name for which famous WWII operation?

source.notes: June 6, 1944. ~160,000 Allied troops landed on five beaches in Normandy, France.

questions.q: Operation Barbarossa was the German code name for the 1941 invasion of which country?

source.notes: Launched June 22, 1941. Largest military operation in history. Named after Frederick Barbarossa.

questions.q: The V1 and V2 were German what?

source.notes: V1 was a pulse-jet cruise missile; V2 was a ballistic rocket. 'V' stood for 'Vergeltungswaffe' (Vengeance Weapon).

questions.q: In which year did D-Day (the Allied invasion of Normandy) take place?

source.notes: June 6, 1944. Commanded by Eisenhower, who later became the 34th President.

questions.q: At which 1945 conference did the Allies set the terms for Japanese surrender?

source.notes: Held July-August 1945. Attended by Truman, Churchill (then Attlee), and Stalin.

questions.q: Which British Midlands city was heavily bombed by German air raids in November 1940?

source.notes: Operation Moonlight Sonata. Coventry Cathedral destroyed. 500+ killed.

questions.q: What was the British name for the period between Britain's declaration of war and the start of major military action?

source.notes: Lasted September 1939 to April 1940. Germans called it 'Sitzkrieg.'

questions.q: Who was the German general nicknamed 'The Desert Fox' for his campaigns in North Africa?

source.notes: Erwin Rommel commanded the Afrika Korps. Forced to commit suicide in 1944 after the July 20 Plot.

questions.q: In which month did President Franklin D. Roosevelt die suddenly in 1945?

source.notes: Died April 12, 1945 from a cerebral hemorrhage. Truman succeeded him.

questions.q: The code name 'Tube Alloys' referred to the British effort to develop what?

source.notes: British WWII atomic bomb research, eventually merged with the US Manhattan Project.

questions.q: Which Japanese city was the target of the first atomic bomb on August 6, 1945?

source.notes: 'Little Boy' dropped from the B-29 'Enola Gay.' Nagasaki bombed August 9.

questions.q: What was the July 20 Plot of 1944?

source.notes: Claus von Stauffenberg planted a briefcase bomb at Hitler's Wolf's Lair. Hitler survived.

questions.q: PT-109, the patrol torpedo boat rammed and sunk by a Japanese destroyer in 1943, was commanded by which future U.S. President?

source.notes: Kennedy swam several miles towing an injured crewmate. Decorated with Navy and Marine Corps Medal.
